authors_text: Lei Guo,Jinyu Zhang,Li Tang,Tong Chen,Lei Zhu,Hongzhi Yin
title: Time Interval-enhanced Graph Neural Network for Shared-account Cross-domain Sequential Recommendation
title_zh: 用于共享账户跨域顺序推荐的时间间隔增强图神经网络
comment: 15 pages, 6 figures
comments: 15 pages, 6 figures
abstract: Shared-account Cross-domain Sequential Recommendation (SCSR) task aims to recommend the next item via leveraging the mixed user behaviors in multiple domains. It is gaining immense research attention as more and more users tend to sign up on different platforms and share accounts with others to access domain-specific services. Existing works on SCSR mainly rely on mining sequential patterns via Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)-based models, which suffer from the following limitations: 1) RNN-based methods overwhelmingly target discovering sequential dependencies in single-user behaviors. They are not expressive enough to capture the relationships among multiple entities in SCSR. 2) All existing methods bridge two domains via knowledge transfer in the latent space, and ignore the explicit cross-domain graph structure. 3) None existing studies consider the time interval information among items, which is essential in the sequential recommendation for characterizing different items and learning discriminative representations for them. In this work, we propose a new graph-based solution, namely TiDA-GCN, to address the above challenges. Specifically, we first link users and items in each domain as a graph. Then, we devise a domain-aware graph convolution network to learn userspecific node representations. To fully account for users' domainspecific preferences on items, two effective attention mechanisms are further developed to selectively guide the message passing process. Moreover, to further enhance item- and account-level representation learning, we incorporate the time interval into the message passing, and design an account-aware self-attention module for learning items' interactive characteristics. Experiments demonstrate the superiority of our proposed method from various aspects.
summary: Shared-account Cross-domain Sequential Recommendation (SCSR) task aims to recommend the next item via leveraging the mixed user behaviors in multiple domains. It is gaining immense research attention as more and more users tend to sign up on different platforms and share accounts with others to access domain-specific services. Existing works on SCSR mainly rely on mining sequential patterns via Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)-based models, which suffer from the following limitations: 1) RNN-based methods overwhelmingly target discovering sequential dependencies in single-user behaviors. They are not expressive enough to capture the relationships among multiple entities in SCSR. 2) All existing methods bridge two domains via knowledge transfer in the latent space, and ignore the explicit cross-domain graph structure. 3) None existing studies consider the time interval information among items, which is essential in the sequential recommendation for characterizing different items and learning discriminative representations for them. In this work, we propose a new graph-based solution, namely TiDA-GCN, to address the above challenges. Specifically, we first link users and items in each domain as a graph. Then, we devise a domain-aware graph convolution network to learn userspecific node representations. To fully account for users' domainspecific preferences on items, two effective attention mechanisms are further developed to selectively guide the message passing process. Moreover, to further enhance item- and account-level representation learning, we incorporate the time interval into the message passing, and design an account-aware self-attention module for learning items' interactive characteristics. Experiments demonstrate the superiority of our proposed method from various aspects.
abstract_zh: 共享账户跨域顺序推荐（SCSR）任务旨在通过利用多个域中的混合用户行为来推荐下一个项目。随着越来越多的用户倾向于在不同的平台上注册并与其他人共享帐户以访问特定领域的服务，它正在获得巨大的研究关注。 SCSR 的现有工作主要依赖于通过基于循环神经网络（RNN）的模型来挖掘顺序模式，该模型存在以下局限性：1）基于 RNN 的方法绝大多数目标是发现单用户行为中的顺序依赖关系。它们的表达能力不足以捕获 SCSR 中多个实体之间的关系。 2）所有现有方法通过潜在空间中的知识转移来桥接两个域，并忽略显式的跨域图结构。 3）现有的研究没有考虑项目之间的时间间隔信息，这对于表征不同项目和学习它们的判别表示的顺序推荐至关重要。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新的基于图的解决方案，即 TiDA-GCN，来解决上述挑战。具体来说，我们首先将每个域中的用户和项目链接为图表。然后，我们设计了一个领域感知图卷积网络来学习用户特定的节点表示。为了充分考虑用户对项目的特定领域偏好，进一步开发了两种有效的注意机制来有选择地指导消息传递过程。此外，为了进一步增强项目和帐户级别的表示学习，我们将时间间隔纳入消息传递中，并设计了一个帐户感知的自注意力模块来学习项目的交互特征。实验从各个方面证明了我们提出的方法的优越性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于时间间隔增强的图神经网络（TiDA-GCN），用于解决共享账户跨领域序列推荐（SCSR）任务，通过建模多领域用户行为和时间信息提升推荐效果。
AI.motivation: 现有SCSR方法依赖RNN模型，难以捕捉多实体关系、忽略显式跨领域图结构且未考虑时间间隔信息，导致推荐效果受限。
AI.method: 构建领域内用户-物品图，设计领域感知图卷积网络（GCN）学习用户表示；引入双注意力机制筛选消息传递路径；融合时间间隔信息并开发账户感知自注意力模块增强表征学习。
AI.result: 实验验证了TiDA-GCN在多个数据集上的优越性，尤其在跨领域推荐准确率和序列建模能力方面显著优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 通过整合图结构、领域感知机制与时间间隔信息，TiDA-GCN有效解决了SCSR任务中的核心挑战，为跨领域推荐提供了新思路。

authors_text: Yuto Nishimura,Yuki Saito,Shinnosuke Takamichi,Kentaro Tachibana,Hiroshi Saruwatari
title: Acoustic Modeling for End-to-End Empathetic Dialogue Speech Synthesis Using Linguistic and Prosodic Contexts of Dialogue History
title_zh: 使用对话历史的语言和韵律背景进行端到端移情对话语音合成的声学建模
comment: 5 pages, 3 figures, Accepted for INTERSPEECH2022
comments: 5 pages, 3 figures, Accepted for INTERSPEECH2022
abstract: We propose an end-to-end empathetic dialogue speech synthesis (DSS) model that considers both the linguistic and prosodic contexts of dialogue history. Empathy is the active attempt by humans to get inside the interlocutor in dialogue, and empathetic DSS is a technology to implement this act in spoken dialogue systems. Our model is conditioned by the history of linguistic and prosody features for predicting appropriate dialogue context. As such, it can be regarded as an extension of the conventional linguistic-feature-based dialogue history modeling. To train the empathetic DSS model effectively, we investigate 1) a self-supervised learning model pretrained with large speech corpora, 2) a style-guided training using a prosody embedding of the current utterance to be predicted by the dialogue context embedding, 3) a cross-modal attention to combine text and speech modalities, and 4) a sentence-wise embedding to achieve fine-grained prosody modeling rather than utterance-wise modeling. The evaluation results demonstrate that 1) simply considering prosodic contexts of the dialogue history does not improve the quality of speech in empathetic DSS and 2) introducing style-guided training and sentence-wise embedding modeling achieves higher speech quality than that by the conventional method.
summary: We propose an end-to-end empathetic dialogue speech synthesis (DSS) model that considers both the linguistic and prosodic contexts of dialogue history. Empathy is the active attempt by humans to get inside the interlocutor in dialogue, and empathetic DSS is a technology to implement this act in spoken dialogue systems. Our model is conditioned by the history of linguistic and prosody features for predicting appropriate dialogue context. As such, it can be regarded as an extension of the conventional linguistic-feature-based dialogue history modeling. To train the empathetic DSS model effectively, we investigate 1) a self-supervised learning model pretrained with large speech corpora, 2) a style-guided training using a prosody embedding of the current utterance to be predicted by the dialogue context embedding, 3) a cross-modal attention to combine text and speech modalities, and 4) a sentence-wise embedding to achieve fine-grained prosody modeling rather than utterance-wise modeling. The evaluation results demonstrate that 1) simply considering prosodic contexts of the dialogue history does not improve the quality of speech in empathetic DSS and 2) introducing style-guided training and sentence-wise embedding modeling achieves higher speech quality than that by the conventional method.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种端到端的移情对话语音合成（DSS）模型，该模型考虑了对话历史的语言和韵律背景。同理心是人类在对话中积极尝试进入对话者内部的行为，而同理心 DSS 是一种在口语对话系统中实现这一行为的技术。我们的模型以语言和韵律特征的历史为条件，用于预测适当的对话上下文。因此，它可以被视为传统的基于语言特征的对话历史建模的延伸。为了有效地训练同理心 DSS 模型，我们研究了 1）使用大型语音语料库预训练的自监督学习模型，2）使用对话上下文嵌入预测的当前话语的韵律嵌入进行风格引导训练，3）结合文本和语音模态的跨模式注意，以及 4）句子嵌入以实现细粒度韵律建模而不是话语建模。评估结果表明：1）仅考虑对话历史的韵律上下文并不能提高共情 DSS 中的语音质量；2）引入风格引导训练和逐句嵌入建模可以实现比传统方法更高的语音质量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于对话历史语言和韵律上下文的端到端共情对话语音合成模型。
AI.motivation: 传统对话语音合成模型仅考虑语言特征，难以捕捉对话中的共情需求。通过引入韵律上下文和风格引导训练，可提升模型对对话情感和语境的建模能力。
AI.method: 采用自监督预训练模型、风格引导训练（结合韵律嵌入）、跨模态注意力机制（融合文本与语音）及句级嵌入建模，替代传统话语级韵律建模。
AI.result: 单纯引入韵律上下文未提升语音质量，但结合风格引导训练与句级嵌入的方法显著优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 共情对话语音合成需结合风格引导训练与细粒度韵律建模，跨模态注意力和预训练技术是关键优化方向。

Empathy is the active attempt by humans to get inside the interlocutor in dialogue, and empathetic DSS is a technology to implement this act in spoken dialogue systems. authors_text: Mustafa B. Yaldiz,Andreas Meuleman,Hyeonjoong Jang,Hyunho Ha,Min H. Kim
title: DeepFormableTag: End-to-end Generation and Recognition of Deformable Fiducial Markers
title_zh: DeepFormableTag：可变形基准标记的端到端生成和识别
comment: ef:ACM Transactions on Graphics 40, 4, Article 67 (August 2021)
comments: ef:ACM Transactions on Graphics 40, 4, Article 67 (August 2021)
abstract: Fiducial markers have been broadly used to identify objects or embed messages that can be detected by a camera. Primarily, existing detection methods assume that markers are printed on ideally planar surfaces. Markers often fail to be recognized due to various imaging artifacts of optical/perspective distortion and motion blur. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel deformable fiducial marker system that consists of three main parts: First, a fiducial marker generator creates a set of free-form color patterns to encode significantly large-scale information in unique visual codes. Second, a differentiable image simulator creates a training dataset of photorealistic scene images with the deformed markers, being rendered during optimization in a differentiable manner. The rendered images include realistic shading with specular reflection, optical distortion, defocus and motion blur, color alteration, imaging noise, and shape deformation of markers. Lastly, a trained marker detector seeks the regions of interest and recognizes multiple marker patterns simultaneously via inverse deformation transformation. The deformable marker creator and detector networks are jointly optimized via the differentiable photorealistic renderer in an end-to-end manner, allowing us to robustly recognize a wide range of deformable markers with high accuracy. Our deformable marker system is capable of decoding 36-bit messages successfully at ~29 fps with severe shape deformation. Results validate that our system significantly outperforms the traditional and data-driven marker methods. Our learning-based marker system opens up new interesting applications of fiducial markers, including cost-effective motion capture of the human body, active 3D scanning using our fiducial markers' array as structured light patterns, and robust augmented reality rendering of virtual objects on dynamic surfaces.

Primarily, existing detection methods assume that markers are printed on ideally planar surfaces. Second, a differentiable image simulator creates a training dataset of photorealistic scene images with the deformed markers, being rendered during optimization in a differentiable manner. The rendered images include realistic shading with specular reflection, optical distortion, defocus and motion blur, color alteration, imaging noise, and shape deformation of markers. Lastly, a trained marker detector seeks the regions of interest and recognizes multiple marker patterns simultaneously via inverse deformation transformation. The deformable marker creator and detector networks are jointly optimized via the differentiable photorealistic renderer in an end-to-end manner, allowing us to robustly recognize a wide range of deformable markers with high accuracy. Our deformable marker system is capable of decoding 36-bit messages successfully at ~29 fps with severe shape deformation. Results validate that our system significantly outperforms the traditional and data-driven marker methods. Our learning-based marker system opens up new interesting applications of fiducial markers, including cost-effective motion capture of the human body, active 3D scanning using our fiducial markers' array as structured light patterns, and robust augmented reality rendering of virtual objects on dynamic surfaces.
abstract_zh: 基准标记已广泛用于识别物体或嵌入可由相机检测到的消息。主要地，现有的检测方法假设标记被印刷在理想的平面上。由于光学/透视畸变和运动模糊的各种成像伪影，标记通常无法被识别。为了克服这些限制，我们提出了一种新颖的可变形基准标记系统，该系统由三个主要部分组成：首先，基准标记生成器创建一组自由形式的颜色图案，以独特的视觉代码对大规模信息进行编码。其次，可微图像模拟器创建具有变形标记的真实感场景图像的训练数据集，并在优化期间以可微方式进行渲染。渲染的图像包括具有镜面反射的真实阴影、光学畸变、散焦和运动模糊、颜色改变、成像噪声和标记的形状变形。最后，训练有素的标记检测器寻找感兴趣的区域并通过逆变形变换同时识别多个标记模式。可变形标记创建器和检测器网络通过可微分的真实感渲染器以端到端的方式联合优化，使我们能够以高精度稳健地识别各种可变形标记。我们的可变形标记系统能够以约 29 fps 成功解码具有严重形状变形的 36 位消息。结果验证我们的系统显着优于传统的数据驱动标记方法。我们基于学习的标记系统开辟了基准标记新的有趣应用，包括经济高效的人体运动捕捉、使用我们的基准标记阵列作为结构光图案的主动 3D 扫描，以及动态表面上虚拟对象的强大增强现实渲染。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种端到端的可变形基准标记生成与识别系统，解决传统标记在光学失真和运动模糊下的识别难题。
AI.motivation: 传统基准标记依赖理想平面假设，在现实场景中因光学失真、运动模糊等问题导致识别失败，需更鲁棒的解决方案。
AI.method: 系统包含三部分：1) 生成器创建自由形式彩色编码；2) 可微图像模拟器生成含失真（如运动模糊、光学畸变）的训练数据；3) 检测器通过逆变形转换同时识别多标记，三者通过可微渲染器端到端优化。
AI.result: 系统在严重形变下以约29fps成功解码36位信息，显著优于传统方法，并支持人体运动捕捉、3D扫描等新应用。
AI.conclusion: 该学习驱动的系统有效解决了传统标记的局限性，为动态场景下的基准标记应用提供了新方向。

authors_text: Yuanfeng Ji,Haotian Bai,Jie Yang,Chongjian Ge,Ye Zhu,Ruimao Zhang,Zhen Li,Lingyan Zhang,Wanling Ma,Xiang Wan,Ping Luo
title: AMOS: A Large-Scale Abdominal Multi-Organ Benchmark for Versatile Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: AMOS：多功能医学图像分割的大规模腹部多器官基准
abstract: Despite the considerable progress in automatic abdominal multi-organ segmentation from CT/MRI scans in recent years, a comprehensive evaluation of the models' capabilities is hampered by the lack of a large-scale benchmark from diverse clinical scenarios. Constraint by the high cost of collecting and labeling 3D medical data, most of the deep learning models to date are driven by datasets with a limited number of organs of interest or samples, which still limits the power of modern deep models and makes it difficult to provide a fully comprehensive and fair estimate of various methods. To mitigate the limitations, we present AMOS, a large-scale, diverse, clinical dataset for abdominal organ segmentation. AMOS provides 500 CT and 100 MRI scans collected from multi-center, multi-vendor, multi-modality, multi-phase, multi-disease patients, each with voxel-level annotations of 15 abdominal organs, providing challenging examples and test-bed for studying robust segmentation algorithms under diverse targets and scenarios. We further benchmark several state-of-the-art medical segmentation models to evaluate the status of the existing methods on this new challenging dataset. We have made our datasets, benchmark servers, and baselines publicly available, and hope to inspire future research. Information can be found at https://amos22.grand-challenge.org.
summary: Despite the considerable progress in automatic abdominal multi-organ segmentation from CT/MRI scans in recent years, a comprehensive evaluation of the models' capabilities is hampered by the lack of a large-scale benchmark from diverse clinical scenarios. Constraint by the high cost of collecting and labeling 3D medical data, most of the deep learning models to date are driven by datasets with a limited number of organs of interest or samples, which still limits the power of modern deep models and makes it difficult to provide a fully comprehensive and fair estimate of various methods. To mitigate the limitations, we present AMOS, a large-scale, diverse, clinical dataset for abdominal organ segmentation. AMOS provides 500 CT and 100 MRI scans collected from multi-center, multi-vendor, multi-modality, multi-phase, multi-disease patients, each with voxel-level annotations of 15 abdominal organs, providing challenging examples and test-bed for studying robust segmentation algorithms under diverse targets and scenarios. We further benchmark several state-of-the-art medical segmentation models to evaluate the status of the existing methods on this new challenging dataset. We have made our datasets, benchmark servers, and baselines publicly available, and hope to inspire future research. Information can be found at https://amos22.grand-challenge.org.
abstract_zh: 尽管近年来 CT/MRI 扫描自动腹部多器官分割取得了相当大的进展，但由于缺乏来自不同临床场景的大规模基准，对模型能力的全面评估受到阻碍。受收集和标记 3D 医学数据成本高昂的限制，迄今为止大多数深度学习模型都是由感兴趣器官或样本数量有限的数据集驱动，这仍然限制了现代深度模型的能力，并且难以对各种方法提供完全全面和公平的估计。为了减轻这些限制，我们提出了 AMOS，这是一个用于腹部器官分割的大规模、多样化的临床数据集。 AMOS 提供从多中心、多供应商、多模态、多阶段、多疾病患者收集的 500 个 CT 和 100 个 MRI 扫描，每个扫描都有 15 个腹部器官的体素级注释，为研究不同目标和场景下的稳健分割算法提供了具有挑战性的示例和测试平台。我们进一步对几种最先进的医学分割模型进行基准测试，以评估现有方法在这个新的具有挑战性的数据集上的状态。我们已经公开了我们的数据集、基准服务器和基线，并希望能够激发未来的研究。信息可在 https://amos22.grand-challenge.org 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AMOS，一个大规模腹部多器官分割的医学图像基准数据集，包含500例CT和100例MRI扫描数据，用于评估分割模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有医学图像分割模型因缺乏大规模、多样化的临床数据集，难以全面评估其性能，限制了方法的比较和改进。
AI.method: 收集多中心、多厂商、多模态的腹部CT/MRI扫描数据，标注15个器官的体素级标签，并对多种先进分割模型进行基准测试。
AI.result: 构建了包含500例CT和100例MRI的AMOS数据集，并公开基准测试服务器和基线模型，为分割算法研究提供挑战性测试平台。
AI.conclusion: AMOS填补了医学图像分割领域大规模多样化数据的空白，可推动鲁棒分割算法的发展，并为未来研究提供标准化评估框架。

authors_text: Xueliang Wang,Jiajun Chen,Feng Wu,Jie Wang
title: Exploiting Global Semantic Similarities in Knowledge Graphs by Relational Prototype Entities
title_zh: 通过关系原型实体利用知识图中的全局语义相似性
comment: 11 pages, 6 figures, Knowledge Graph Embedding
comments: 11 pages, 6 figures, Knowledge Graph Embedding
abstract: Knowledge graph (KG) embedding aims at learning the latent representations for entities and relations of a KG in continuous vector spaces. An empirical observation is that the head (tail) entities connected by the same relation often share similar semantic attributes -- specifically, they often belong to the same category -- no matter how far away they are from each other in the KG; that is, they share global semantic similarities. However, many existing methods derive KG embeddings based on the local information, which fail to effectively capture such global semantic similarities among entities. To address this challenge, we propose a novel approach, which introduces a set of virtual nodes called \textit{\textbf{relational prototype entities}} to represent the prototypes of the head and tail entities connected by the same relations. By enforcing the entities' embeddings close to their associated prototypes' embeddings, our approach can effectively encourage the global semantic similarities of entities -- that can be far away in the KG -- connected by the same relation. Experiments on the entity alignment and KG completion tasks demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms recent state-of-the-arts.
summary: Knowledge graph (KG) embedding aims at learning the latent representations for entities and relations of a KG in continuous vector spaces. An empirical observation is that the head (tail) entities connected by the same relation often share similar semantic attributes -- specifically, they often belong to the same category -- no matter how far away they are from each other in the KG; that is, they share global semantic similarities. However, many existing methods derive KG embeddings based on the local information, which fail to effectively capture such global semantic similarities among entities. To address this challenge, we propose a novel approach, which introduces a set of virtual nodes called \textit{\textbf{relational prototype entities}} to represent the prototypes of the head and tail entities connected by the same relations. By enforcing the entities' embeddings close to their associated prototypes' embeddings, our approach can effectively encourage the global semantic similarities of entities -- that can be far away in the KG -- connected by the same relation. Experiments on the entity alignment and KG completion tasks demonstrate that our approach significantly outperforms recent state-of-the-arts.
abstract_zh: 知识图（KG）嵌入旨在学习连续向量空间中知识图谱的实体和关系的潜在表示。经验观察发现，相同关系连接的头（尾）实体往往具有相似的语义属性——具体来说，它们往往属于同一类别——无论它们在知识图谱中彼此相距多远；也就是说，它们具有全局语义相似性。然而，许多现有方法基于局部信息推导知识图谱嵌入，无法有效捕获实体之间的全局语义相似性。为了解决这个挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的方法，它引入了一组称为 \textit{\textbf{关系原型实体}} 的虚拟节点来表示由相同关系连接的头实体和尾实体的原型。通过强制实体的嵌入接近其关联原型的嵌入，我们的方法可以有效地促进通过相同关系连接的实体的全局语义相似性（这些实体可能在 KG 中很远）。实体对齐和 KG 完成任务的实验表明，我们的方法明显优于最新的技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过引入关系原型实体来捕捉知识图谱中实体全局语义相似性的嵌入方法。
AI.motivation: 现有知识图谱嵌入方法依赖局部信息，无法有效捕捉同一关系下相隔较远实体间的全局语义相似性。
AI.method: 引入虚拟节点'关系原型实体'表示相同关系连接的头尾实体原型，通过约束实体嵌入接近对应原型嵌入来强化全局语义相似性。
AI.result: 在实体对齐和知识图谱补全任务中显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: 关系原型实体方法有效解决了知识图谱中全局语义相似性建模难题，验证了其有效性。

authors_text: Tao Wang,Xiumei Chen,Xiaoling Zhang,Shuoling Zhou,Qianjin Feng,Meiyan Huang
title: Multi-View Imputation and Cross-Attention Network Based on Incomplete Longitudinal and Multimodal Data for Conversion Prediction of Mild Cognitive Impairment
title_zh: 基于不完整纵向和多模态数据的多视图插补和交叉注意网络用于轻度认知障碍的转换预测
abstract: Predicting whether subjects with mild cognitive impairment (MCI) will convert to Alzheimer's disease is a significant clinical challenge. Longitudinal variations and complementary information inherent in longitudinal and multimodal data are crucial for MCI conversion prediction, but persistent issue of missing data in these data may hinder their effective application. Additionally, conversion prediction should be achieved in the early stages of disease progression in clinical practice, specifically at baseline visit (BL). Therefore, longitudinal data should only be incorporated during training to capture disease progression information. To address these challenges, a multi-view imputation and cross-attention network (MCNet) was proposed to integrate data imputation and MCI conversion prediction in a unified framework. First, a multi-view imputation method combined with adversarial learning was presented to handle various missing data scenarios and reduce imputation errors. Second, two cross-attention blocks were introduced to exploit the potential associations in longitudinal and multimodal data. Finally, a multi-task learning model was established for data imputation, longitudinal classification, and conversion prediction tasks. When the model was appropriately trained, the disease progression information learned from longitudinal data can be leveraged by BL data to improve MCI conversion prediction at BL. MCNet was tested on two independent testing sets and single-modal BL data to verify its effectiveness and flexibility in MCI conversion prediction. Results showed that MCNet outperformed several competitive methods. Moreover, the interpretability of MCNet was demonstrated. Thus, our MCNet may be a valuable tool in longitudinal and multimodal data analysis for MCI conversion prediction. Codes are available at https://github.com/Meiyan88/MCNET.
summary: Predicting whether subjects with mild cognitive impairment (MCI) will convert to Alzheimer's disease is a significant clinical challenge. Longitudinal variations and complementary information inherent in longitudinal and multimodal data are crucial for MCI conversion prediction, but persistent issue of missing data in these data may hinder their effective application. Additionally, conversion prediction should be achieved in the early stages of disease progression in clinical practice, specifically at baseline visit (BL). Therefore, longitudinal data should only be incorporated during training to capture disease progression information. To address these challenges, a multi-view imputation and cross-attention network (MCNet) was proposed to integrate data imputation and MCI conversion prediction in a unified framework. First, a multi-view imputation method combined with adversarial learning was presented to handle various missing data scenarios and reduce imputation errors. Second, two cross-attention blocks were introduced to exploit the potential associations in longitudinal and multimodal data. Finally, a multi-task learning model was established for data imputation, longitudinal classification, and conversion prediction tasks. When the model was appropriately trained, the disease progression information learned from longitudinal data can be leveraged by BL data to improve MCI conversion prediction at BL. MCNet was tested on two independent testing sets and single-modal BL data to verify its effectiveness and flexibility in MCI conversion prediction. Results showed that MCNet outperformed several competitive methods. Moreover, the interpretability of MCNet was demonstrated. Thus, our MCNet may be a valuable tool in longitudinal and multimodal data analysis for MCI conversion prediction. Codes are available at https://github.com/Meiyan88/MCNET.
abstract_zh: 预测患有轻度认知障碍 (MCI) 的受试者是否会转变为阿尔茨海默病是一项重大的临床挑战。纵向和多模态数据固有的纵向变化和补充信息对于 MCI 转换预测至关重要，但这些数据中持续存在的数据缺失问题可能会阻碍其有效应用。此外，在临床实践中，应在疾病进展的早期阶段实现转化预测，特别是在基线访视（BL）时。因此，纵向数据仅应在训练期间纳入以捕获疾病进展信息。为了应对这些挑战，提出了多视图插补和交叉注意网络（MCNet），将数据插补和 MCI 转换预测集成在统一的框架中。首先，提出了一种与对抗性学习相结合的多视图插补方法，以处理各种缺失数据场景并减少插补错误。其次，引入两个交叉注意块来利用纵向和多模态数据中的潜在关联。最后，针对数据插补、纵向分类和转换预测任务建立了多任务学习模型。当模型经过适当的训练后，BL 数据可以利用从纵向数据中学习到的疾病进展信息来改进 BL 的 MCI 转化预测。 MCNet在两个独立的测试集和单模态BL数据上进行了测试，验证其在MCI转化预测方面的有效性和灵活性。结果表明 MCNet 优于多种竞争方法。此外，还证明了MCNet的可解释性。因此，我们的 MCNet 可能是用于 MCI 转换预测的纵向和多模态数据分析的有价值的工具。代码可在 https://github.com/Meiyan88/MCNET 获取。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于多视角插补和交叉注意力网络（MCNet）的方法，用于利用不完整的纵向和多模态数据预测轻度认知障碍（MCI）向阿尔茨海默病的转化。
AI.motivation: 纵向和多模态数据中的纵向变化及互补信息对MCI转化预测至关重要，但数据缺失问题限制了其应用。同时，临床需在疾病早期（基线访问）进行预测，需在训练中整合纵向数据以捕捉疾病进展信息。
AI.method: 提出MCNet框架：1）结合对抗学习的多视角插补方法处理缺失数据；2）引入两个交叉注意力模块挖掘纵向与多模态数据关联；3）构建多任务学习模型，联合数据插补、纵向分类和转化预测任务。
AI.result: MCNet在两个独立测试集和单模态基线数据上均优于多种竞争方法，且模型具有可解释性。
AI.conclusion: MCNet为纵向和多模态数据分析提供了有效工具，可提升MCI转化预测的准确性与实用性。

search_text: Multi-View Imputation and Cross-Attention Network Based on Incomplete Longitudinal and Multimodal Data for Conversion Prediction of Mild Cognitive Impairment 基于不完整纵向和多模态数据的多视图插补和交叉注意网络用于轻度认知障碍的转换预测 Predicting whether subjects with mild cognitive impairment (MCI) will convert to Alzheimer's disease is a significant clinical challenge. Longitudinal variations and complementary information inherent in longitudinal and multimodal data are crucial for MCI conversion prediction, but persistent issue of missing data in these data may hinder their effective application. Additionally, conversion prediction should be achieved in the early stages of disease progression in clinical practice, specifically at baseline visit (BL). Therefore, longitudinal data should only be incorporated during training to capture disease progression information. To address these challenges, a multi-view imputation and cross-attention network (MCNet) was proposed to integrate data imputation and MCI conversion prediction in a unified framework. First, a multi-view imputation method combined with adversarial learning was presented to handle various missing data scenarios and reduce imputation errors. Second, two cross-attention blocks were introduced to exploit the potential associations in longitudinal and multimodal data. Finally, a multi-task learning model was established for data imputation, longitudinal classification, and conversion prediction tasks. When the model was appropriately trained, the disease progression information learned from longitudinal data can be leveraged by BL data to improve MCI conversion prediction at BL. MCNet was tested on two independent testing sets and single-modal BL data to verify its effectiveness and flexibility in MCI conversion prediction. Results showed that MCNet outperformed several competitive methods. Moreover, the interpretability of MCNet was demonstrated. Thus, our MCNet may be a valuable tool in longitudinal and multimodal data analysis for MCI conversion prediction. Codes are available at https://github.com/Meiyan88/MCNET. 预测患有轻度认知障碍 (MCI) 的受试者是否会转变为阿尔茨海默病是一项重大的临床挑战。纵向和多模态数据固有的纵向变化和补充信息对于 MCI 转换预测至关重要，但这些数据中持续存在的数据缺失问题可能会阻碍其有效应用。此外，在临床实践中，应在疾病进展的早期阶段实现转化预测，特别是在基线访视（BL）时。因此，纵向数据仅应在训练期间纳入以捕获疾病进展信息。为了应对这些挑战，提出了多视图插补和交叉注意网络（MCNet），将数据插补和 MCI 转换预测集成在统一的框架中。首先，提出了一种与对抗性学习相结合的多视图插补方法，以处理各种缺失数据场景并减少插补错误。其次，引入两个交叉注意块来利用纵向和多模态数据中的潜在关联。最后，针对数据插补、纵向分类和转换预测任务建立了多任务学习模型。当模型经过适当的训练后，BL 数据可以利用从纵向数据中学习到的疾病进展信息来改进 BL 的 MCI 转化预测。 MCNet在两个独立的测试集和单模态BL数据上进行了测试，验证其在MCI转化预测方面的有效性和灵活性。结果表明 MCNet 优于多种竞争方法。此外，还证明了MCNet的可解释性。因此，我们的 MCNet 可能是用于 MCI 转换预测的纵向和多模态数据分析的有价值的工具。代码可在 https://github.com/Meiyan88/MCNET 获取。 该论文提出了一种基于多视角插补和交叉注意力网络（MCNet）的方法，用于利用不完整的纵向和多模态数据预测轻度认知障碍（MCI）向阿尔茨海默病的转化。 Tao Wang,Xiumei Chen,Xiaoling Zhang,Shuoling Zhou,Qianjin Feng,Meiyan Huang eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Omar Elharrouss,Younes Akbari,Noor Almaadeed,Somaya Al-Maadeed
title: Backbones-Review: Feature Extraction Networks for Deep Learning and Deep Reinforcement Learning Approaches
title_zh: Backbones-Review：深度学习和深度强化学习方法的特征提取网络
abstract: To understand the real world using various types of data, Artificial Intelligence (AI) is the most used technique nowadays. While finding the pattern within the analyzed data represents the main task. This is performed by extracting representative features step, which is proceeded using the statistical algorithms or using some specific filters. However, the selection of useful features from large-scale data represented a crucial challenge. Now, with the development of convolution neural networks (CNNs), the feature extraction operation has become more automatic and easier. CNNs allow to work on large-scale size of data, as well as cover different scenarios for a specific task. For computer vision tasks, convolutional networks are used to extract features also for the other parts of a deep learning model. The selection of a suitable network for feature extraction or the other parts of a DL model is not random work. So, the implementation of such a model can be related to the target task as well as the computational complexity of it. Many networks have been proposed and become the famous networks used for any DL models in any AI task. These networks are exploited for feature extraction or at the beginning of any DL model which is named backbones. A backbone is a known network trained in many other tasks before and demonstrates its effectiveness. In this paper, an overview of the existing backbones, e.g. VGGs, ResNets, DenseNet, etc, is given with a detailed description. Also, a couple of computer vision tasks are discussed by providing a review of each task regarding the backbones used. In addition, a comparison in terms of performance is also provided, based on the backbone used for each task.

In addition, a comparison in terms of performance is also provided, based on the backbone used for each task.
abstract_zh: 为了使用各种类型的数据来了解现实世界，人工智能（AI）是当今最常用的技术。主要任务是在分析数据中找到模式。这是通过提取代表性特征步骤来执行的，该步骤使用统计算法或使用一些特定的过滤器来进行。然而，从大规模数据中选择有用的特征是一个严峻的挑战。现在，随着卷积神经网络（CNN）的发展，特征提取操作变得更加自动化和容易。 CNN 可以处理大规模数据，并涵盖特定任务的不同场景。对于计算机视觉任务，卷积网络还用于为深度学习模型的其他部分提取特征。选择合适的网络进行特征提取或深度学习模型的其他部分并不是随机的工作。因此，这种模型的实现可能与目标任务及其计算复杂度有关。许多网络已经被提出，并成为用于任何人工智能任务中的任何深度学习模型的著名网络。这些网络用于特征提取或在任何称为骨干网的深度学习模型的开头。主干网络是一个已知的网络，之前接受过许多其他任务的训练，并证明了其有效性。在本文中，概述了现有的骨干网，例如VGGs、ResNets、DenseNet等都给出了详细的描述。此外，还通过对每个任务所使用的主干进行回顾来讨论几个计算机视觉任务。此外，还根据每个任务使用的主干网提供性能比较。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了用于深度学习和深度强化学习的特征提取网络（即主干网络），并比较了不同任务中常用主干网络的性能。
AI.motivation: 随着深度学习的发展，选择适合特定任务且计算复杂度适中的主干网络成为关键挑战，需系统梳理现有网络并分析其适用性。
AI.method: 通过回顾经典主干网络（如VGG、ResNet、DenseNet等），结合计算机视觉任务案例，分析其设计特点并对比性能表现。
AI.result: 总结了主流主干网络的结构特性，讨论了其在图像分类、目标检测等任务中的应用，并提供了性能对比分析。
AI.conclusion: 主干网络的选择需综合考虑任务需求与计算成本，未来研究可进一步探索轻量化与高性能的平衡方案。

authors_text: Sigal Raab,Inbal Leibovitch,Peizhuo Li,Kfir Aberman,Olga Sorkine-Hornung,Daniel Cohen-Or
title: MoDi: Unconditional Motion Synthesis from Diverse Data
title_zh: MoDi：来自各种数据的无条件运动合成
comment: Video: https://youtu.be/O1sVzwrsNUg, Project page: https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi, Code: https://github.com/sigal-raab/MoDi
comments: Video: https://youtu.be/O1sVzwrsNUg, Project page: https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi, Code: https://github.com/sigal-raab/MoDi
abstract: The emergence of neural networks has revolutionized the field of motion synthesis. Yet, learning to unconditionally synthesize motions from a given distribution remains challenging, especially when the motions are highly diverse. In this work, we present MoDi -- a generative model trained in an unsupervised setting from an extremely diverse, unstructured and unlabeled dataset. During inference, MoDi can synthesize high-quality, diverse motions. Despite the lack of any structure in the dataset, our model yields a well-behaved and highly structured latent space, which can be semantically clustered, constituting a strong motion prior that facilitates various applications including semantic editing and crowd simulation. In addition, we present an encoder that inverts real motions into MoDi's natural motion manifold, issuing solutions to various ill-posed challenges such as completion from prefix and spatial editing. Our qualitative and quantitative experiments achieve state-of-the-art results that outperform recent SOTA techniques. Code and trained models are available at https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi.
summary: The emergence of neural networks has revolutionized the field of motion synthesis. Yet, learning to unconditionally synthesize motions from a given distribution remains challenging, especially when the motions are highly diverse. In this work, we present MoDi -- a generative model trained in an unsupervised setting from an extremely diverse, unstructured and unlabeled dataset. During inference, MoDi can synthesize high-quality, diverse motions. Despite the lack of any structure in the dataset, our model yields a well-behaved and highly structured latent space, which can be semantically clustered, constituting a strong motion prior that facilitates various applications including semantic editing and crowd simulation. In addition, we present an encoder that inverts real motions into MoDi's natural motion manifold, issuing solutions to various ill-posed challenges such as completion from prefix and spatial editing. Our qualitative and quantitative experiments achieve state-of-the-art results that outperform recent SOTA techniques. Code and trained models are available at https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi.
abstract_zh: 神经网络的出现彻底改变了运动合成领域。然而，学习无条件地合成给定分布的运动仍然具有挑战性，特别是当运动高度多样化时。在这项工作中，我们提出了 MoDi——一种在无监督的环境中从极其多样化、非结构化和未标记的数据集训练的生成模型。在推理过程中，MoDi 可以合成高质量、多样化的运动。尽管数据集中缺乏任何结构，我们的模型产生了一个行为良好且高度结构化的潜在空间，它可以在语义上聚类，构成强大的运动先验，促进包括语义编辑和人群模拟在内的各种应用。此外，我们还提出了一种编码器，可将真实运动反转为 MoDi 的自然运动流形，为各种不适的挑战提供解决方案，例如完成前缀和空间编辑。我们的定性和定量实验取得了最先进的结果，优于最新的 SOTA 技术。代码和训练模型可在 https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi 获取。

AI.tldr: 提出一种无监督生成模型MoDi，用于从多样化、无结构数据中合成高质量且多样的运动。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以处理高度多样化的运动数据，且缺乏结构化潜在空间以支持语义编辑等应用。
AI.method: 采用无监督训练构建结构化潜在空间，并设计编码器将真实运动映射到该空间，实现运动补全与编辑。
AI.result: 实验结果在定性和定量指标上均优于最新技术，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: MoDi模型在无监督学习下生成高质量运动，潜在空间具备语义结构，编码器有效解决逆问题，适用于多种应用。

search_text: MoDi: Unconditional Motion Synthesis from Diverse Data MoDi：来自各种数据的无条件运动合成 The emergence of neural networks has revolutionized the field of motion synthesis. Yet, learning to unconditionally synthesize motions from a given distribution remains challenging, especially when the motions are highly diverse. In this work, we present MoDi -- a generative model trained in an unsupervised setting from an extremely diverse, unstructured and unlabeled dataset. During inference, MoDi can synthesize high-quality, diverse motions. Despite the lack of any structure in the dataset, our model yields a well-behaved and highly structured latent space, which can be semantically clustered, constituting a strong motion prior that facilitates various applications including semantic editing and crowd simulation. In addition, we present an encoder that inverts real motions into MoDi's natural motion manifold, issuing solutions to various ill-posed challenges such as completion from prefix and spatial editing. Our qualitative and quantitative experiments achieve state-of-the-art results that outperform recent SOTA techniques. Code and trained models are available at https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi. 神经网络的出现彻底改变了运动合成领域。然而，学习无条件地合成给定分布的运动仍然具有挑战性，特别是当运动高度多样化时。在这项工作中，我们提出了 MoDi——一种在无监督的环境中从极其多样化、非结构化和未标记的数据集训练的生成模型。在推理过程中，MoDi 可以合成高质量、多样化的运动。尽管数据集中缺乏任何结构，我们的模型产生了一个行为良好且高度结构化的潜在空间，它可以在语义上聚类，构成强大的运动先验，促进包括语义编辑和人群模拟在内的各种应用。此外，我们还提出了一种编码器，可将真实运动反转为 MoDi 的自然运动流形，为各种不适的挑战提供解决方案，例如完成前缀和空间编辑。我们的定性和定量实验取得了最先进的结果，优于最新的 SOTA 技术。代码和训练模型可在 https://sigal-raab.github.io/MoDi 获取。 提出一种无监督生成模型MoDi，用于从多样化、无结构数据中合成高质量且多样的运动。 Sigal Raab,Inbal Leibovitch,Peizhuo Li,Kfir Aberman,Olga Sorkine-Hornung,Daniel Cohen-Or cs.GR,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Jogendra Nath Kundu,Akshay Kulkarni,Suvaansh Bhambri,Deepesh Mehta,Shreyas Kulkarni,Varun Jampani,R. Venkatesh Babu
title: Balancing Discriminability and Transferability for Source-Free Domain Adaptation
title_zh: 平衡无源域适应的可辨别性和可转移性
comment: ICML 2022. Project page: https://sites.google.com/view/mixup-sfda
comments: ICML 2022. Project page: https://sites.google.com/view/mixup-sfda
abstract: Conventional domain adaptation (DA) techniques aim to improve domain transferability by learning domain-invariant representations; while concurrently preserving the task-discriminability knowledge gathered from the labeled source data. However, the requirement of simultaneous access to labeled source and unlabeled target renders them unsuitable for the challenging source-free DA setting. The trivial solution of realizing an effective original to generic domain mapping improves transferability but degrades task discriminability. Upon analyzing the hurdles from both theoretical and empirical standpoints, we derive novel insights to show that a mixup between original and corresponding translated generic samples enhances the discriminability-transferability trade-off while duly respecting the privacy-oriented source-free setting. A simple but effective realization of the proposed insights on top of the existing source-free DA approaches yields state-of-the-art performance with faster convergence. Beyond single-source, we also outperform multi-source prior-arts across both classification and semantic segmentation benchmarks.
summary: Conventional domain adaptation (DA) techniques aim to improve domain transferability by learning domain-invariant representations; while concurrently preserving the task-discriminability knowledge gathered from the labeled source data. However, the requirement of simultaneous access to labeled source and unlabeled target renders them unsuitable for the challenging source-free DA setting. The trivial solution of realizing an effective original to generic domain mapping improves transferability but degrades task discriminability. Upon analyzing the hurdles from both theoretical and empirical standpoints, we derive novel insights to show that a mixup between original and corresponding translated generic samples enhances the discriminability-transferability trade-off while duly respecting the privacy-oriented source-free setting. A simple but effective realization of the proposed insights on top of the existing source-free DA approaches yields state-of-the-art performance with faster convergence. Beyond single-source, we also outperform multi-source prior-arts across both classification and semantic segmentation benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 传统的域适应（DA）技术旨在通过学习域不变表示来提高域可迁移性；同时保留从标记的源数据收集的任务可区分性知识。然而，同时访问标记源和未标记目标的要求使得它们不适合具有挑战性的无源 DA 设置。实现有效的原始域到通用域映射的简单解决方案提高了可转移性，但降低了任务的可区分性。通过从理论和实证的角度分析这些障碍，我们得出了新颖的见解，表明原始样本和相应的翻译通用样本之间的混合增强了可辨别性与可转移性的权衡，同时适当尊重以隐私为导向的无源设置。在现有的无源 DA 方法之上简单而有效地实现所提出的见解，可以产生最先进的性能和更快的收敛速度。除了单源之外，我们在分类和语义分割基准方面也优于多源现有技术。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种在无源域数据情况下平衡判别性与迁移性的领域自适应方法。
AI.motivation: 传统领域自适应方法需要同时访问标记源域和未标记目标域数据，无法满足源域不可用的挑战性场景需求。
AI.method: 通过混合原始样本与对应的翻译通用样本，优化判别性-迁移性权衡，同时符合隐私保护的源域不可用设定。
AI.result: 在单源和多源场景下均取得分类与语义分割任务的最先进性能，且收敛速度更快。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决了源域不可用场景下的领域自适应难题，实现了判别性与迁移性的最优平衡。
